mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which drives aggregate demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS changes are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces monetary stimulus through unconventional channels, sharply lowering discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining unemployment creates a joint demand-labor virtuous cycle where income growth validates consumption expansion, amplifying equity earnings expectations and producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS changes and UNRATE changes exhibits a rank correlation with 5-day SPX returns below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high unemployment regimes, decreases in the unemployment rate signal diminishing labor market slack and reduced recession risk, triggering a compression in equity risk premiums and positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, decreases in UNRATE do not correspond to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing momentum that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following INDPRO crossing above its 12-month average are negative in more than 45% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising new residential housing starts signal accelerating construction activity and household formation, which boosts cyclical demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and expected future real rates, which generates negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes fail to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a rank IC above 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of negative real GDP growth and a federal funds rate below 1% signals a severe demand shortfall that overwhelms monetary easing, leading to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations collapse.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter while FEDFUNDS is below 1%
regime_hint: demand contraction with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS below 1% does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.
